notes: Native Windows baseline records physical GetAsyncKeyState(Shift) acknowledgement after KeyDown and a separate after-KeyUp/readback cleanup state. Shift is used instead of F12 to avoid text mutation while exercising the same held-key SendInput path. The runtime fix disables Enigo's drop-time held-key release only for explicit KeyDown calls; KeyUp, KeyPress, KeyChord, ReleaseAll, and shutdown still release explicitly.
